skill.name: create-pr
skill.description: Create pull requests that link related GitHub issues, use closing keywords, and include clear change context.
skill.summary: Guides issue-linked GitHub pull request creation with structured descriptions and verification steps.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backtick examples as shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance. Manual review found a documentation-only skill that uses expected git and GitHub CLI commands for PR creation; no malicious code, prompt injection, credential access, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: GitHub CLI Commands Can Change Remote Repository State
low_findings.description: The skill instructs use of git and gh commands to inspect branch changes, list issues, create a pull request, and verify linked issues. This is expected for a pull request workflow, but gh pr create can publish remote repository content and should be reviewed before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit Markdown examples for git and GitHub CLI workflows. They are legitimate for the stated purpose, but gh pr create has real side effects in the connected repository.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Substitution Is Used For PR Body Assembly
low_findings.description: The gh pr create example wraps a static here-document with command substitution to pass a multi-line PR body. I did not find untrusted interpolation, hidden payloads, or command injection evidence; this appears to be a benign Markdown example.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution is visible and only invokes cat on a static here-document template. The pattern is shell-sensitive, but the reviewed context does not show malicious intent or user-controlled shell construction.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Matches Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and reconnaissance hits map to prose, issue-closing keywords, paths, examples, or general architecture language. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation, system enumeration code, credential access, or network exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation text and examples at the flagged locations rather than executable code. Confidence is high enough to dismiss the static matches, with residual caution because the skill still encourages external CLI use.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples In Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains git and gh command examples. They are not bundled scripts or hidden execution paths, but assistants using the skill may run them during a PR workflow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external command pattern is clearly present and directly related to the skill purpose. Review did not find concealed commands or unrelated execution behavior.

content.value_statement: Pull requests often miss related issues, testing notes, and clear context. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured GitHub PR workflow with closing keywords and verification steps.
actual_capabilities: Checks the current branch and related GitHub issues before drafting a PR.
Builds PR descriptions with summaries, key changes, testing notes, and files changed.
Uses GitHub closing keywords such as Closes, Fixes, and Resolves for related issues.
Provides GitHub CLI commands for creating and verifying pull requests.
Recommends Mermaid diagrams when architectural flow or data movement needs explanation.
limitations: Requires a git repository with GitHub CLI installed and authenticated.
Does not prove that linked issues are semantically correct without human review.
Does not run tests unless the assistant or user executes project test commands separately.
Assumes GitHub pull request behavior and issue closing semantics.

use_cases.title: Prepare Feature Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Create a complete PR description that explains the change, links feature issues, and lists validation steps.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team PR Quality
use_cases.description: Apply a repeatable structure for summaries, key changes, testing notes, and issue references across contributors.

use_cases.title: Verify Release Fix Tracking
use_cases.description: Confirm that bug fix pull requests use closing keywords so GitHub updates issue status when merged.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a small branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pull request for the current branch. Summarize the changes, include testing notes, and link any related issues you find.

prompt_templates.title: Link Issues Before Publishing
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect this branch, search for related GitHub issues, and draft a PR that uses closing keywords for fixed issues and related references for context issues.

prompt_templates.title: Draft A Review-Ready PR
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a review-ready PR description with key changes, additional changes, testing notes, architectural impact, files changed, and all related issues.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare A Complex Architecture PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with diagrams and impact analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the branch against the main branch, identify related issues, draft a concise PR, and include a Mermaid diagram if it clarifies the changed flow.

output_examples.input: A branch fixes token expiration and updates authentication tests.
output: PR title: fix: Resolve token expiration handling
Summary explains the session timeout problem and the token refresh solution.
Related Issues lists fixed issues with closing keywords and separate context issues.
Testing notes include manual authentication checks and automated test coverage.

output_examples.input: A feature branch adds support for larger account balances.
output: PR title: feat: Implement large balance support
Key Changes lists data model, validation, and display updates.
Architectural Impact explains how balance handling changes across services.
Files Changed highlights the main modules reviewers should inspect.

output_examples.input: A refactor changes database connection structure.
output: PR title: refactor: Restructure database connections
Description focuses on reliability and maintainability instead of implementation noise.
A diagram is suggested when the new connection flow affects several components.

best_practices: Review every issue reference before publishing the PR.
Use closing keywords only for issues that the PR actually resolves.
Keep the summary short, then provide testing and architecture details in dedicated sections.
anti_patterns: Creating a PR with a vague title and no issue references.
Using closing keywords for issues that are only partially related.
Adding diagrams or file lists that do not help reviewers understand the change.

faq.question: Does this skill create the pull request automatically?
faq.answer: It provides GitHub CLI commands that can create a PR when executed in an authenticated repository.

faq.question: Does it require GitHub CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow uses gh commands for issue lookup, PR creation, and link verification.

faq.question: Can it close GitHub issues on merge?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides the PR body to use GitHub closing keywords such as Closes, Fixes, and Resolves.

faq.question: Does it decide which issues are safe to close?
faq.answer: It can search and suggest related issues, but the user should verify each issue before publishing.

faq.question: Can it include architecture diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends Mermaid diagrams for flow changes, API interactions, state machines, and data models.

faq.question: Is it limited to Claude?
faq.answer: No. The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
